message:
fix(packs/database/redis): close compound-command bypass via safe keywords

The redis pack's safe patterns matched generic keywords (GET, SCAN,
INFO, KEYS, DBSIZE, CONFIG GET) anywhere in the command. Because the
pack runs whenever a destructive keyword (FLUSHALL, FLUSHDB, DEBUG,
etc.) is present, a compound command like:

  redis-cli FLUSHALL && redis-cli GET key
  redis-cli 'FLUSHDB; GET foo'
  redis-cli CONFIG SET dir /tmp; redis-cli SCAN 0
  redis-cli DEBUG SEGFAULT; redis-cli INFO

would match the safe GET/SCAN/INFO pattern, short-circuit the pack at
the cross-pack safe check, and never reach the destructive pattern that
was about to block the FLUSHALL / CONFIG SET / DEBUG.

Each safe pattern now carries a start-anchored negative lookahead that
refuses to match when any destructive redis keyword also appears in the
command:

  ^(?!.*\b(?:FLUSHALL|FLUSHDB|DEBUG|SHUTDOWN|CONFIG\s+(?:SET|REWRITE))\b).*<safe-kw>\b

The `^` anchor is critical: without it, fancy_regex evaluates the
lookahead at each start position, so a match starting after the
destructive keyword would satisfy "no FLUSHALL from here onward" and
still short-circuit.

Added tests for both the bypass (must be blocked) and the happy path
(standalone redis-cli GET/SCAN/INFO must remain safe).
